ingredients.name: a few sprigs of fresh coriander

preparationSteps: Squeeze the sausagemeat out of the skin into little balls (if using veggie sausages, roughly slice) and place in a large deep casserole pan over a medium-high heat with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, the cinnamon stick and a big pinch of black pepper. Fry for 5 minutes, or until browned, stirring regularly, then strip in the rosemary leaves and stir in the Cajun seasoning and smoked paprika for 1 minute.
Go in with the frozen mixed veg and a pinch of sea salt and pepper. Cook for 10 minutes, or until softened and lightly golden, stirring occasionally.

Peel, deseed and chop the butternut squash into 1.5cm chunks, then peel and finely chop the garlic. Add everything to the pan along with the balsamic vinegar, stirring for 1 minute, then add the beans (juices and all).
Preheat the oven to 180°C/350°F/gas 4. Tip in the tomatoes, breaking them up with the back of your spoon, then half-fill each tin with water, swirl around and pour into the pan.

Bring to the boil, then cover with a lid and bake in the oven for 1 hour 30 minutes, or until the squash is tender and the sauce is slightly thickened, removing the lid for the final 30 minutes and adding splashes of water, if needed.

Once the time’s up, gently mash around a third of the chilli, until some of the beans are squashed into the sauce, then give everything a good stir. Taste and season to perfection, tweaking with a little balsamic vinegar and extra virgin olive oil, if you like. Pick over the coriander leaves and serve with fluffy rice, dollops of yoghurt and a fresh green salad.

name: Xmas Cauliflower Cheese
preparationSteps: Rinse and cut the cauliflower up into medium size florets and cut the ends of the leaves.
Add the florets and leaves into roasting trays, add some oil, salt and pepper, then roast in an oven at 200 C / 390 F for 20-25 minutes until starting to brown.

Meanwhile, prepare the cheese sauce. Melt the butter in a large pan then add the garlic. Cook over a medium heat until the butter and garlic have both browned (make sure not to burn the garlic otherwise it will go bitter). Add the flour, then mix together and cook for a few minutes to make the roux.
Gradually add in the cold milk, continually whisking, until all combined. Bring to a gentle bubble and cook for a few more minutes.
Remove the sauce from the heat, add in the remaining ingredients then stir though until melted.
Preheat an oven to 180 C / 355 F.
Add the roasted cauliflower and leaves into a wide dish, then cover with the cheese sauce. Mix until the sauce is distributed evenly, then top with more cheddar.
Place into the preheated oven and cook for 30 minutes until starting browned on top. If needed, place under the grill to quickly speed this up if more browning is needed after 30 minutes.

name: Udon Noodle Soup

ingredients.rawIngredient: Mushrooms: 200g (e.g., shiitake, button, or oyster), sliced
ingredients.name: Mushrooms: 200g (e.g., shiitake, button, or oyster)

ingredients.rawIngredient: Optional: Spinach or Bok Choy, Carrot slices, Soft-boiled Egg, Seaweed (Nori), Chilli flakes
ingredients.name: Optional: Spinach or Bok Choy, Carrot slices, Soft-boiled Egg, Seaweed (Nori), Chilli flakes

preparationSteps: In a large pot, heat sesame oil over medium heat. Add minced garlic and grated ginger, sauté until fragrant.
Add the homemade chicken stock and bring to a gentle simmer.
Cook the Mushrooms:
In a separate pan, lightly sauté the mushrooms until they're just cooked. Set aside.
In a small bowl, mix miso paste with a bit of warm water to create a smooth mixture.
Turn the heat under the chicken stock to low. Stir in the miso paste mixture and soy sauce. Ensure the broth doesn’t boil after adding miso to preserve its flavour and probiotics.
Add the shredded chicken and cooked mushrooms to the broth.
In a separate pot, cook the udon noodles according to the package instructions until just tender. Drain and rinse with cold water to stop the cooking process.
Place the cooked udon noodles in serving bowls.
Pour the hot soup over the noodles.
Top with spring onions, and any optional ingredients like spinach, carrot slices, a soft-boiled egg, nori, or a sprinkle of chilli flakes.
Serve hot, ensuring each bowl gets a good mix of noodles, chicken, mushrooms, and broth.

name: Air Fryer Marinated Pork Tenderloin
note: You might think that sticking meat in the air fryer would dry it out, but this Air Fryer Marinated Pork Tenderloin proves just the opposite. The results are nothing short of perfect. The sweet, savory, and flavor-rich marinade gives the tenderloin a slightly crisp and salty exterior that’s the perfect complement to the juicy, tender interior. It’s surprisingly reminiscent of a grilled marinated pork tenderloin. Feel free to use any marinade you love, but be sure it includes some sweetness so the exterior will caramelize. The honey in our marinade, for example, aids caramelization to yield the tastiest end product. Flipping the pork throughout the cooking process guarantees even browning. Leftovers are great chopped and incorporated into fried rice or barbecue for sandwiches.
preparationSteps: Trim silver skin and any excess fat from tenderloins, and place in a large ziplock plastic bag or shallow dish.

Whisk together honey, soy sauce, Dijon, olive oil, lime juice, garlic, ginger, and Sriracha in a small bowl. Pour marinade over pork; seal bag and marinate in refrigerator at least 1 hour and up to 24 hours.

Preheat air-fryer to 400°F for 2 minutes. Lightly coat air fryer basket with cooking spray.  Place pork tenderloins in air fryer basket; discard marinade. Cook 18 to 20 minutes or until desired degree of doneness, flipping once or twice during cooking process. Let rest 5 to 10 minutes before slicing.

name: Glazed ham with a fig and apple chutney glaze
note:
Tips
Air Fryer Size: Ensure your air fryer is big enough to fit the ham without crowding.
Glaze Variations: Feel free to adjust the glaze ingredients to your taste. More honey will make it sweeter, and more mustard will add a bit of tang.
Serving Suggestion: Serve with roasted vegetables or a fresh salad.
preparationSteps: Make the Glaze: Mix the fig and apple chutney, honey, Dijon mustard, and ground cloves in a bowl. Set aside.

Prepare the Ham: If the ham is not pre-cooked, you'll need to boil it first. Boil for about 20-30 minutes until it reaches an internal temperature of about 63°C (145°F). If it's pre-cooked, you can skip this step.
Preheat the Air Fryer: Set your air fryer to 180°C (350°F) and let it preheat for a few minutes.
Glazing the Ham: Brush a generous amount of the glaze over the ham. Make sure to cover all sides.
Air Frying the Ham: Place the ham in the air fryer basket. Cook for about 15-20 minutes. Halfway through, open the air fryer and apply another layer of glaze.
Check for Doneness: The ham should reach an internal temperature of 70°C (160°F) to ensure it's fully heated through. Use a meat thermometer to check.
Rest and Serve: Once cooked, let the ham rest for a few minutes before slicing. This helps retain the juices.
Optional: If you prefer a crispier outer layer, you can increase the temperature to 200°C (390°F) for the last 5 minutes of cooking.

name: 🐟 Pan-Fried Salmon with Creamy Lemon Orzo
note: Pan-fried salmon, nestled in a bed of creamy orzo with lemon and parmesan. So quick to make and all cooked in one pan!

ingredients.rawIngredient: zest and juice of 1/2 a lemon
ingredients.name: zest and juice of 1/2 a lemon

preparationSteps: Place the salmon fillets on a plate.
Mix together the salt, garlic salt, pepper, paprika, and dried parsley. Sprinkle onto the top of the salmon and pat it down, so it sticks.
Heat the oil and butter in a large frying pan (skillet), over a medium-high heat.
Add the salmon fillets, skin-side-up (so the part coated with the spice mix is in the oil).
Cook for 3-4 minutes, until the salmon is golden brown, then remove the salmon from the pan and place on a plate, skin-side-down (we'll continue cooking it shortly).
Turn the heat down to medium and add the garlic to the pan. Cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly, so the garlic doesn’t burn.
Add the dried orzo to the pan, stir with the garlic, then add the chicken stock and stir again.
Nestle the salmon fillets into the orzo, skin-side down.

Turn the heat back up to medium-high and bring to the boil, then turn the heat down and simmer for 8-9 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the orzo is al dente (cooked, but with a little bite). You'll need to move the salmon with a set of tongs to stir the orzo underneath, so it doesn't stick to the pan.
Add the cream, parmesan, lemon zest and juice, salt, and pepper to the orzo and stir together.
Turn off the heat, sprinkle on the chopped parsley and a little black pepper. Arrange the lemon wedges in the pan, then serve.

name: John Lockes pancakes
note: Makes approximately 10 8-inch pancakes

ingredients.rawIngredient: 3 whole eggs plus 4 additional egg yolks
ingredients.name: whole eggs plus 4 additional egg yolks

preparationSteps: First, melt the butter. Set it aside.
Put the whole eggs and egg yolks in a large bowl. Beat with a whisk or hand-held mixer until well combined.

Add the flour and beat until smooth and completely combined. Add 6 Tablespoons of the cream to the egg flour mixture and mix until combined. While stirring or beating, pour in the melted butter. Add the remaining cream and orange blossom water and stir to combine. Grate 1/2 a nutmeg and stir into batter.
Heat a frying pan or skillet on a high heat until a drop of water skitters across the surface. Lower the heat to medium.

Pour approximately half a cup of batter into the center of the pan and spread by swirling the pan to create an 8-inch pancake. Cook for 1 minute or until the edges of the pancake lift and appear lacy and the middle looks mostly set. Flip the pancake and cook for an additional 30 seconds.
Repeat until your batter is gone. Serve the pancakes immediately.

name: Thai Salmon Curry
note:
### Tips:

- **Curry Paste:** Panang curry paste's spiciness can vary by brand, so start with less and add more according to your taste.
- **Coconut Milk:** For a richer sauce, choose full-fat coconut milk. If you prefer a lighter version, light coconut milk works well too.
- **Fish Sauce and Sugar:** Balance is key in Thai cooking. Adjust the fish sauce and sugar to find your perfect balance of salty and sweet.

This Thai Salmon Panang Curry is a beautifully flavorful dish that brings a touch of Thai cuisine to your table, suitable for an adventurous weeknight dinner or a special occasion. Enjoy the process of creating this aromatic, creamy curry that pairs perfectly with the rich, tender salmon.

ingredients.rawIngredient: - **1-2 tablespoons Panang curry paste** (adjust based on your spice preference)
ingredients.name: **1-2 tablespoons Panang curry paste** (adjust based on your spice preference)

ingredients.rawIngredient: - **2 kaffir lime leaves**, torn (or substitute with a little lime zest if unavailable)
ingredients.name: **2 kaffir lime leaves**, torn (or substitute with a little lime zest if unavailable)

ingredients.rawIngredient: - **1 tablespoon fish sauce**, or to taste
ingredients.name: **1 tablespoon fish sauce**, or to taste

ingredients.rawIngredient: - **1 teaspoon brown sugar**, or to taste
ingredients.name: **1 teaspoon brown sugar**, or to taste

ingredients.rawIngredient: - **Optional: vegetables** such as bell peppers, snap peas, or baby corn, sliced
ingredients.name: **Optional: vegetables** such as bell peppers, snap peas, or baby corn

ingredients.rawIngredient: - **To serve: Jasmine rice**, cooked according to package instructions
ingredients.name: **To serve: Jasmine rice**, cooked according to package instructions

preparationSteps:
**Prepare the Salmon:**

Pat the salmon fillets dry with paper towels. Season lightly with salt on both sides.

**Panang Curry Sauce:**

Heat the oil in a large skillet or pan over medium heat. Add the Panang curry paste and fry for a minute until fragrant, stirring constantly to prevent burning.

Slowly pour in the coconut milk, stirring well to combine the curry paste evenly throughout the milk. Add the torn kaffir lime leaves.

Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then add the fish sauce and brown sugar. Stir well and taste, adjusting the seasoning as needed.

**Cooking the Salmon:**

Add the salmon fillets to the pan, skin-side down (if they have skin). Spoon some of the sauce over the tops of the fillets.

Cover the pan with a lid and let the salmon cook for about 5-7 minutes, or until it's just cooked through. If you're adding vegetables, add them around the salmon at this point, ensuring they're submerged in the sauce so they cook quickly.

**Final Touches:**

Once the salmon is cooked and the vegetables are tender (if using), sprinkle Thai basil leaves over the curry. Give the sauce a gentle stir to mix in the basil.

**Serve:**

Carefully remove the salmon fillets and place them on a serving dish or individual plates. Spoon the curry sauce (and vegetables) over and around the salmon.

Serve hot with jasmine rice on the side.

name: Korean Beef Bolgogi
note:
Tips:

	•	Freezing the Beef: If you’re slicing the beef yourself, partially freezing it for about 30 minutes can make it easier to cut into very thin slices.
	•	Alternative Cooking Methods: Bulgogi is also great cooked on a grill for a more authentic flavor and char.

This Beef Bulgogi recipe brings a piece of Korean culinary tradition into your home, offering a deliciously marinated dish that balances sweet, savory, and umami flavors. Enjoy cooking and sharing this beloved dish with family and friends.

ingredients.rawIngredient: •	1 medium pear or apple, grated (this adds natural sweetness and helps tenderize the meat)
ingredients.name: medium pear or apple, grated (this adds natural sweetness and helps tenderize the meat)

ingredients.rawIngredient: •	500g (approximately 1 pound) thinly sliced beef (sirloin or ribeye are excellent choices)
ingredients.name: (approximately 1 pound) thinly sliced beef (sirloin or ribeye are excellent choices)

ingredients.rawIngredient: •	1 carrot, julienned (optional for added crunch and color)
ingredients.name: carrot, julienned (optional for added crunch and color)

ingredients.rawIngredient: •	Sesame seeds and additional chopped green onions (for garnish)
ingredients.name: Sesame seeds and additional chopped green onions (for garnish)

preparationSteps:
Prepare the Marinade:

In a large mixing bowl, combine soy sauce, sugar, sesame oil, minced garlic, grated ginger, grated pear or apple, chopped green onions, rice wine, and black pepper. Stir until the sugar has mostly dissolved.

Marinate the Beef:

Add the thinly sliced beef to the marinade. Mix well to ensure each piece is evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, though marinating overnight will yield the best flavor.

Prepare the Vegetables:

While the beef marinates, slice the onion and julienne the carrot. Set aside.

Cook the Bulgogi:

Heat a tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Remove the beef from the marinade, shaking off excess (but do not discard the marinade), and cook in batches to avoid crowding the pan. Each batch should take about 3-4 minutes to cook through and get slightly caramelized edges.

After all the beef is cooked, quickly stir-fry the sliced onion and carrot in the same pan, adding a bit more oil if needed. You can add a few tablespoons of the remaining marinade to the vegetables for extra flavor.

Combine and Serve:

Return the cooked beef to the pan with the vegetables, stirring everything together for another minute or two.

Garnish with sesame seeds and additional chopped green onions.

Serving Suggestions:

Serve the Bulgogi hot with steamed rice and a side of kimchi or a fresh salad for a complete meal.

name: French Fries
preparationSteps:
**Preparation:**

Cut potatoes into strips, approximately 1/4 to 1/2 inch thick. Rinse in cold water to remove excess starch, then dry thoroughly with a towel. Moisture on the surface can cause the oil to splatter.

Heat peanut oil in a wok. You'll need enough oil to fully submerge the fries, typically 2 to 3 inches deep. Use a deep-frying thermometer to monitor the oil's temperature.

**First Fry (Blanching):**

Heat the oil to a lower temperature, around 300°F (150°C). This is for cooking the fries through without browning them.

Add the fries in batches to avoid overcrowding, which can lower the oil temperature too much and result in soggy fries.

Fry for about 4-5 minutes, or until they're soft but not coloured. Remove the fries and drain them on a rack or paper towels. Let them cool for at least 10 minutes or up to several hours.

**Second Fry (Crisping):**

Increase the oil temperature to 375°F (190°C). The higher temperature will crisp up the fries.

Return the fries to the oil in batches, frying until they're golden brown and crispy, about 3-5 minutes.

Remove the fries from the oil, drain on a rack or paper towels, and season with salt or your choice of seasonings while they're still hot.

The key to success with twice-fried French fries is managing the temperature of the oil and ensuring the fries are dry before the first fry. This technique maximises the texture and flavour, making for a delicious side dish or snack.

name: Rhubarb crumble
note: Make this classic rhubarb crumble for an easy, family-friendly dessert. Use seasonal rhubarb and a handful of ingredients to make a stunning pud.

ingredients.rawIngredient: 500g rhubarb, chopped into chunks the length of your thumb
ingredients.name: rhubarb, chopped into chunks the length of your thumb

preparationSteps: Tip 500g thumb-length chunks of rhubarb into a saucepan with 100g golden caster sugar and 3 tbsp port, if using.
Cover and simmer on a very low heat for 15 mins, adding more sugar if you want. When soft (but still holding its shape) and sweet enough, pour the rhubarb into a medium baking dish.
Heat oven to 200C/180C fan/gas 6.
To make the topping, rub 140g self-raising flour and 85g chilled butter together with your fingers until you have a soft, crumbly topping.
Now add 50g light brown muscovado sugar and 50g chopped walnuts, if using. Mix together with your hands.
Scatter the topping over the rhubarb and bake for 30 mins or until golden brown on top.
Serve piping hot with a big jug of thick vanilla custard. Refer to the tip for a recipe.

preparationSteps: Heat the oven to 220C/200C fan/gas 7. Rub a little oil over each potato and put on the top shelf of the oven. Bake for 20 mins, then turn down the oven to 190C/170C fan/ gas 5 and bake for 45 mins-1 hr more until the skin is crisp and the inside soft. Make a cross in the centre of each potato, dollop over the soured cream and serve.

note: Serve with Thai jasmine rice for a flavour-packed low-fat meal

Drizzle the lime juice on top and then scatter the pieces of pak choi around and on top of the fish. Pour the soy sauce over the pak choi and loosely seal the foil to make a package, making sure you leave space at the top for the steam to circulate as the fish cooks.
Steam for 15 minutes. (If you haven’t got a steamer, put the parcel on a heatproof plate over a pan of gently simmering water, cover with a lid and steam.)

name: Korean Vegetable Pancakes
note: Bursting with tasty vegetables and tangy kimchi, these Korean pancakes make a quick and simple weeknight dinner. They’re great for lunchboxes and on picnics, too!

ingredients.rawIngredient: ½ cup potato starch or cornflour (cornstarch)
ingredients.name: potato starch or cornflour (cornstarch)

ingredients.rawIngredient: 2 cups finely julienned mixed vegetables (e.g. carrots, zucchini, capsicum)
ingredients.name: finely julienned mixed vegetables (e.g. carrots, zucchini, capsicum)

ingredients.rawIngredient: 5 spring onions (scallions), thinned out lengthways and cut into roughly 4cm batons
ingredients.name: spring onions (scallions), thinned out lengthways and cut into roughly 4cm batons

preparationSteps: 1.Preheat oven to 150C/300F.
2.For the dipping sauce, combine the ingredients in a bowl and set aside until ready to serve.

3.For the pancakes, combine the flour, potato starch, baking powder and salt in a large bowl. Mix until well combined. Add the egg and ¾ cup (180ml/6 fl oz) of cold water. Whisk until well combined. Then fold through the kimchi, kimchi juice, vegetables and two-thirds of the spring onion.

4.Heat enough oil to just cover the base of a shallow frying pan over medium-high heat. Scoop ¼ cupfuls of the batter mixture into the pan. Spread out a little with the back of a spoon. Cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden and the batter is cooked through. You’ll need to do this in batches, so transfer cooked pancakes to a baking tray, season with a little more salt and keep warm in the oven while you cook the rest.
5.Sprinkle with remaining spring onion. Serve with dipping sauce.

name: Rice Hob
preparationSteps: Bring the pot to a boil over high heat.
Once it starts boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot with the lid, and let it simmer.
Cook for about 15-20 minutes, or until the water is absorbed and the rice is tender.
After cooking, remove the pot from the heat and let it sit, covered, for about 5-10 minutes to allow the steam to finish the cooking process.
After that, fluff the rice with a fork before serving. Enjoy your meal!

name: Peanut butter yogurt choc treats

ingredients.rawIngredient: 2 tbsp maple syrup or other sweeter
ingredients.name: maple syrup or other sweeter

preparationSteps: Mix together yogurt, peanut butter, and maple syrup (or honey) until well combined.
Portion out the mixture into scoops and place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Freeze for 1-2 hours until firm.
Melt dark chocolate with a bit of coconut oil until smooth and creamy.
Dip each frozen scoop into the melted chocolate, ensuring it's fully coated. Use a fork to lift it out and let excess chocolate drip off.
Sprinkle crunchy nuts on top of each coated scoop while the chocolate is still wet.
Place the treats back on the baking sheet and return them to the freezer or refrigerator for a few minutes to set. 7. Serve and enjoy your homemade chocolaty treats!

name: one-pan chicken with orzo, courgettes, tomatoes, and lemon
note: 1.5 courgettes
preparationSteps:
**Prepare the chicken:**

Season the chicken breasts with salt and pepper.

In a large ovenproof pan, heat a tablespoon of olive oil over medium-high heat.

Place the chicken breasts skin-side down in the pan and cook until the skin is golden and crispy, about 5-7 minutes. Flip the chicken and cook for another 5 minutes. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

**Sauté the vegetables:**

In the same pan, add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.

Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

Add the sliced courgettes and cook until they start to soften, about 3-4 minutes.

**Deglaze the pan and add orzo:**

Pour in the white wine to deglaze the pan, scraping up any brown bits from the bottom.

Let the wine reduce by half, about 2-3 minutes.

Stir in the orzo pasta and cook for 2 minutes, allowing it to toast slightly.

Add the diced tomatoes and cook for another minute.

**Add the stock, thyme, and lemon:**

Pour in the chicken stock and stir to combine.

Add the fresh thyme sprigs and the zest of one lemon.

Bring the mixture to a simmer.

**Combine and cook:**

Nestle the chicken breasts back into the pan, skin-side up.

Transfer the pan to a preheated oven at 180°C (350°F) and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the orzo is tender.

**Finish with parmesan and lemon juice:**

Remove the pan from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes.

Discard the thyme sprigs.

Stir in the butter and grated parmesan cheese until melted and well combined.

Squeeze the juice of the lemon over the dish.

**Serve and garnish:**

Serve the chicken on top of the orzo mixture.

Garnish with fresh basil leaves and additional parmesan if desired.

name: Creamy Miso Tomato Chicken
note: Eat a perfect fusion of flavours with our Creamy Miso Tomato Chicken. Juicy, skin-on chicken breast infused with rich umami from miso, creating an unforgettable depth of flavour.

ingredients.rawIngredient: 1 tbsp red miso paste (you could also use white or shiro miso)
ingredients.name: red miso paste (you could also use white or shiro miso)

ingredients.rawIngredient: finely grated parmesan cheese, to serve
ingredients.name: finely grated parmesan cheese, to serve

preparationSteps: Preheat oven to 200°C (390°F).
Step 2

Place the cherry tomatoes on a baking tray. Drizzle with 1 tablespoon of olive oil and season with a teaspoon of salt. Mix to coat and then roast in the oven for 15 minutes or until blistered and the tomatoes have collapsed.
Step 3

To prepare the chicken, use a knife to slice off the thin tenderloin piece from the underside of the chicken breast. Save the tenderloins for another use. Use a rolling pin to flatten the thickest end of each piece so the fillets are more even in thickness. Season the chicken with salt and pepper.
Step 4

Place a large frying pan over high heat. When it’s hot, add 1 tablespoon of oil and cook the chicken skin-side down for 6–7 minutes or until the skin is deeply golden. Turn the chicken over and cook for another 2–3 minutes or until lightly coloured. Transfer to a plate.
Step 5

Place the pan back over a medium heat and add the remaining tablespoon of oil to the pan. Add the garlic and cook for a minute until fragrant. Take your tomatoes out of the oven and add them, along with their juices, to the pan. Use a spoon to smoosh the tomatoes a little as they bubble away in the pan for about 2 minutes. Then add in the baby spinach and cook, stirring, until just wilted. Stir through the miso, then stir through the cream. Add the chicken, skin-side up, back into the pan and allow to simmer in the sauce for 5 minutes or until the chicken is just cooked through. Sprinkle over the parsley and parmesan and serve.
Notes
Notes:
You may need to go to a local butcher to source skin-on chicken breasts, as the ones sold in the supermarket are mostly skinless.

name: Tomato And Red Pepper Soup

ingredients.rawIngredient: - 2 red bell peppers, deseeded and quartered
ingredients.name: red bell peppers, deseeded and quartered

ingredients.rawIngredient: - Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
ingredients.name: Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste

preparationSteps: ## Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F).
2. Place the tomatoes, red peppers, onion, and garlic on a large baking tray. Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with thyme, basil, salt, and pepper. Toss to coat evenly.
3. Roast in the oven for about 30-35 minutes, or until the vegetables are soft and slightly charred.
4. Transfer the roasted vegetables to a large pot. Add the tomato paste and stock.
5. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
6. Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until smooth. Alternatively, carefully transfer to a blender in batches.
7. Stir in the balsamic vinegar and adjust seasoning to taste.
8. If using, stir in the cream for a richer soup.
9. Serve hot, garnished with fresh basil leaves and a drizzle of cream if desired.

name: Chicken Gravy
preparationSteps: Leave the juices and fond (browned bits) in the pan.
Add about 2 tablespoons of butter or oil to the pan and melt it over medium heat.
Sprinkle in an equal amount of flour (about 2 tablespoons) and whisk it with the fat to create a roux. Cook this for 1-2 minutes to remove the raw flour taste.
Slowly pour in about 1 to 1.5 cups of chicken broth or milk (or a combination), whisking constantly to prevent lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, stirring frequently. The gravy will thicken as it heats.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. You can also add herbs like thyme or rosemary if desired.

Simmer for a few more minutes until the gravy reaches your desired consistency. If it's too thick, add more liquid; if too thin, let it simmer longer.

This method creates a flavorful gravy that incorporates the chicken drippings for extra taste. Would you like more details on any of these steps?

name: Basmati Rice Simple Way
preparationSteps: Take 60g of basmati rice per person and soak for minimum 30 minutes for best results.
Take water equal to 1.5-2 times the rice quantity and add the soaked rice to its Bring to boil. Reduce heat and cook on medium heat for 10-12 minutes.
Drain well, cover and let stand for 3-5 minutes.
Slightly fork through the cooked rice and the finest basmati rice is now ready to serve.

name: Gigli with chickpeas

ingredients.rawIngredient: 45ml	olive oil, plus extra to serve
ingredients.name: olive oil, plus extra to serve

ingredients.rawIngredient: 25g	anchovy fillets in oil, drained and finely chopped (about 7)
ingredients.name: anchovy fillets in oil

ingredients.rawIngredient: 1	lemon: finely shave the skin of ½, then juice to get 2 tbsp
ingredients.name: lemon: finely shave the skin of ½, then juice to get 2 tbsp

preparationSteps: Put the olive oil into a large sauté pan and place on a high heat. Add the onion, garlic, cumin, thyme, anchovies, lemon skin, ½ teaspoon of salt and a good grind of pepper. Fry for 3–4 minutes, stirring often, until soft and golden. Reduce the heat to medium high, then add the chickpeas and sugar and fry for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chickpeas begin to brown and crisp up. Add the chicken stock and lemon juice and simmer for 6 minutes, until the sauce has reduced slightly. Remove from the heat and set aside. You can make this in advance, if you like, and warm through before serving.
Bring a large pan of salted water to the boil. Add the pasta and cook for 8 minutes, or according to the packet instructions, until al dente. Drain and set aside.

Stir the spinach and parsley into the chickpeas: the residual heat of the sauce should cook the spinach, but if it doesn’t wilt, just warm the chickpeas gently on the stove. Transfer the pasta to the pan of chickpeas and stir to combine. Divide between four bowls and sprinkle the za'atar on top. Finish with a drizzle of oil, and serve.

name: Ger’s Air Fryer Chunky Chips
preparationSteps: Cut Morris Piper chips into chunky size
Steep in cold water for 30m
Drain and add boiling water with salt in pot
Bring to boil and simmer for 3 mins. Move around gently.
Drain and steam dry. Then towel to remove excess water.
Toss in EVO or beef fat and salt
Air fry for 190 for 20m. Then 15 minutes at 205.
Remove and fine salt.
Serve on own or with aged balsamic vinegar.

name: Honey, teriyaki chicken and rice bowls

ingredients.rawIngredient: Salt, pepper, garlic powder to taste
ingredients.name: Salt, pepper, garlic powder to taste

name: Broccoli Wok

ingredients.rawIngredient: 1 tbsp olive oil: 1 tbsp olive oil
ingredients.name: olive oil: 1 tbsp olive oil

ingredients.rawIngredient: 85g/3oz broccoli, cut into florets: 85g/3oz broccoli, cut into florets
ingredients.name: g/3oz broccoli
ingredients.note: cut into florets: 85g/3oz broccoli, cut into florets

ingredients.rawIngredient: 1 garlic clove, chopped: 1 garlic clove, chopped
ingredients.name: garlic clove, chopped: 1 garlic clove

ingredients.rawIngredient: ½ red chilli, finely chopped: ½ red chilli, finely chopped
ingredients.name: red chilli, finely chopped: ½ red chilli

ingredients.rawIngredient: 2 tbsp soy sauce: 2 tbsp soy sauce
ingredients.name: soy sauce: 2 tbsp soy sauce

ingredients.rawIngredient: 1 tsp sesame oil: 1 tsp sesame oil
ingredients.name: sesame oil: 1 tsp sesame oil

ingredients.rawIngredient: ½ lime, juice only: ½ lime, juice only
ingredients.name: lime, juice only: ½ lime, juice only

ingredients.rawIngredient: salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.name: salt and freshly ground black pepper

preparationSteps: Heat the olive oil in a wok, add the broccoli and fry for 2-3 minutes, or until starting to soften, then add the garlic and chilli and fry for 1-2 minutes.
Add the soy sauce, sesame oil, lime juice, salt and freshly ground black pepper and fry for 1-2 minutes, or until the broccoli is tender.
To serve, spoon the stir-fry into a bowl and serve as a side dish.

name: Leftover Roast Chicken Pasta

ingredients.rawIngredient: 1/2 cup white wine or balsamic vinegar
ingredients.name: white wine or balsamic vinegar

preparationSteps: Cook pasta in salted water according to package directions until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add onions and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
If using wine, add it now and let it reduce by half.
Add cherry tomatoes and cook until they start to burst, about 5 minutes.
Add shredded chicken and chicken broth. Simmer for 3-4 minutes to heat through.
Stir in heavy cream and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add cooked pasta to the skillet and toss to combine. If needed, add reserved pasta water to reach desired consistency.
Add spinach and cook until just wilted.
Stir in Parmesan cheese and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with fresh basil leaves and red pepper flakes if desired.

name: Chicken Schnitzel
preparationSteps: Place a layer of cling film on your work surface and pop the chicken fillets on top. Cover with another piece of cling film and, using a rolling pin, bash the chicken until it is 2-3mm thick.
Put the flour on a plate and season, then put the egg on another plate. Dip the chicken in the flour to coat, then into the egg.

Mix together the breadcrumbs and Parmesan in a shallow bowl, then toss the chicken in the mixture to completely coat in the crumbs. Put the chicken on a plate and chill in the fridge until ready to eat if you’re not cooking them straight away.
Recipe continues below advert

Heat the oil in a large frying pan over a fairly high heat and cook the chicken schnitzels two at a time. Sizzle them for 2-3 mins each side until completely golden, then lift out onto kitchen paper to drain. You can keep them warm in a low oven while you cook the rest. Serve with the coleslaw

name: Lamb meatballs in tagliatelle
preparationSteps: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large frying pan over a medium heat. Fry 1 finely chopped onion for 5–7 minutes until softened. Add 2 crushed garlic cloves and 2 teaspoons of dried herbs and fry for a further 2 minutes. Transfer half to a bowl to cool completely.

Add 400g of tinned tomatoes to the pan with a tin of water, breaking up the tomatoes with a wooden spoon. Add 6 halved olives, 2 teaspoons of red wine vinegar and season. Simmer over a low-medium heat for 15 minutes until thickened.

Heat more oil in a frying pan and fry the meatballs over a medium-high heat for 10 minutes, turning often, until golden. Meanwhile, cook 300g of tagliatelle pasta to pack instructions. Drain, reserving a mug of pasta cooking water.
Add the meatballs to the tomato sauce and cook for 6 minutes over a low heat or until cooked through.

Stir 10g of dill through the tomato sauce, then stir in the tagliatelle until the pasta is coated in the sauce, adding splashes of cooking water to loosen. Serve topped with 200g of cheese and 10g of dill.

name: Prawn and Aubergine Rogan Josh
preparationSteps: Heat 2-3 tablespoons of oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for about 5 minutes until soft and translucent. Add minced garlic and cook for another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Next, add your diced aubergine to the pan with a pinch of salt. Fry for about 5 minutes until the aubergine begins to soften and turn golden.
Pour in your jar of Rogan Josh sauce and stir well to coat all ingredients. Let this simmer for about 10 minutes to allow the flavors to develop and the aubergine to fully cook through.
Add your coconut milk and stir to combine, creating a rich and creamy sauce. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Finally, add the prawns and cook for 3-4 minutes until they turn pink and are completely cooked through. Be careful not to overcook them as they can become tough.
You can finish the dish with a squeeze of lime juice if you have it, and some fresh coriander for garnish.
Serve your Prawn and Aubergine Rogan Josh with steamed rice or naan bread to soak up the delicious sauce.
